name: Ensure Functions are not publicly accessible
description: Serverless functions that can be invoked over HTTP without authentication.
body:
### Overview

This control identifies cases where a function exposes an unauthenticated HTTP entry point. The checks cover AWS Lambda Function URLs with `AuthType = NONE`, Azure Functions HTTP triggers with anonymous auth, Alibaba Function Compute HTTP triggers, and Google Cloud Functions or Cloud Run backed functions that allow unauthenticated invocation.

Public functions are internet-facing application endpoints. They should require authentication unless they are intentionally public and protected by application-level controls.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Require authentication for the affected function or remove the public HTTP trigger if it is not needed.

### AWS

For AWS, this control identifies functions when a Lambda Function URL exists and its auth type is `NONE`.

1. Open **AWS Lambda** and select the affected function.
2. Go to **Configuration > Function URL**.
3. Change **Auth type** from `NONE` to `AWS_IAM`, or delete the Function URL if it is not required.
4. Review the function resource-based policy and remove public `lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl` permissions.
5. Test invocation with a SigV4-signed request from an approved identity.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

CLI command:

```bash
aws lambda update-function-url-config --function-name {{asset.idFromProvider}} --auth-type AWS_IAM --region {{asset.region}}
```

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/urls-auth.html

The control is satisfied when the function URL is absent or its auth type is no longer `NONE`.

### GCP

For Google Cloud, this control identifies functions when the function or backing Cloud Run service allows unauthenticated invocation.

1. Open the affected Cloud Function or Cloud Run service.
2. Set authentication to **Require authentication**.
3. Remove `allUsers` from `roles/cloudfunctions.invoker` or `roles/run.invoker`.
4. Grant invoker access only to approved users, groups, or service accounts.
5. Test invocation with an authenticated identity token.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

CLI example for Cloud Run backed functions:

```bash
gcloud run services remove-iam-policy-binding {{asset.name}} --member=allUsers --role=roles/run.invoker --region {{asset.region}}
```

Reference: https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/authenticating/public

The control is satisfied when unauthenticated invocation is disabled.

### Azure

For Azure, this control identifies Functions when an inbound HTTP trigger has `authLevel: anonymous`.

1. Open the Function App and select the affected HTTP-triggered function.
2. Change the trigger authorization level from `anonymous` to `function` or `admin`.
3. Prefer Microsoft Entra authentication, API Management, or private endpoints for production HTTP APIs.
4. Redeploy the function configuration.
5. Test the endpoint without a key or token and confirm it is rejected.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/security-concepts

The control is satisfied when the HTTP trigger no longer has anonymous authorization.

### Alibaba

For Alibaba, this control identifies Function Compute functions when an HTTP trigger exposes the function publicly.

1. Open **Function Compute** and select the affected function.
2. Open the HTTP trigger configuration.
3. Require authentication or remove the HTTP trigger if it is not required.
4. If public access is required, put API Gateway or another authenticated front door in front of the function.
5. Reduce the function role permissions to only the resources it needs.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

Reference: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/functioncompute/fc-3-0/user-guide/trigger-management

The control is satisfied when the function is no longer exposed by an unauthenticated HTTP trigger.
